description: Getting a leanblueprint to deploy with ALL links resolving (Lean dep-graph links + LaTeX cross-refs). (1) dep-graph 'Lean' links point to <pages-url>/docs/find/#doc/<Name> (doc-gen4); they 404 unless CI builds API docs — set `api-docs: true` in the docgen-action step. (2) \uses{...} expands to \vphantom\ref{...}; the dep-graph + PDF cross-refs need >=3 latex passes to converge. latexmk's default 2 passes leaves many 'undefined reference' warnings and CI fails — in blueprint/src/latexmkrc set `$max_repeat = 5;` and `$force_mode = 1;` (force_mode keeps emitting the PDF past transient unresolved-ref warnings). (3) The ACTUAL CI failure is usually a LaTeX parse error mid-file (undefined macro like \re, or `\delta_\inf` where \inf is a \mathop) that aborts pdflatex so the .aux never populates, cascading into dozens of misleading 'undefined reference' warnings. Scan the log for the FIRST real error (`! Undefined control sequence`, `Missing { inserted`), not the ref warnings. Define every macro in macros/common.tex (\re := \operatorname{Re}); use explicit subscript braces (\delta_{\inf}). (4) `leanblueprint checkdecls` reads blueprint/lean_decls, which is REGENERATED by `leanblueprint web` — after renaming a Lean decl, `rm -rf blueprint/web blueprint/lean_decls; leanblueprint web` BEFORE checkdecls, else it reports stale 'X is missing'. (5) gitignore generated artifacts: blueprint/web/, blueprint/lean_decls, blueprint/src/*.paux (and *.aux/*.log/*.toc/*.pdf under src/). (6) Local pre-push loop: `leanblueprint pdf` then `grep -c undefined blueprint/print/print.log` must be 0 (after the passes); `leanblueprint web` then `leanblueprint checkdecls` must exit 0. (7) \leanok goes on a theorem/proof whose Lean proof BODY is sorry-free (even if it calls a sorried lemma); the dep-graph then colors that node by its own status while the leaves stay open.
